channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Carrie Underwood responds to 'American Idol' boos amid political speculation
item.description: Carrie Underwood, the country music superstar and judge on ‘American Idol,’ has remained unapologetic despite receiving boos from the audience during a recent episode. The backlash occurred after she ...

item.title: Iran Conflict Challenges UK's Inflation Forecast
item.description: The ongoing war in Iran has dramatically impacted global energy markets, with wholesale gas prices in the UK surging. This rise is particularly alarming because gas prices are a key determinant of ele...

item.title: Fertiliser shortages due to Strait of Hormuz closure could impact food prices
item.description: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East, particularly the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, is threatening global supply chains, especially fertiliser imports vital to Australian agriculture. Australia...

item.title: Man fatally shot by police after assaulting two women in Potts Point
item.description: In a tragic incident in Sydney’s Potts Point, a man was shot and killed by police after allegedly assaulting two women inside an apartment building. The man, aged in his late 30s or early 40s, was con...

item.title: Samsung Solve for Tomorrow 2026 Opens for Public School Learners Across South Africa
item.description: Samsung South Africa is calling on Grade 10 and 11 public school learners to register for the 2026 Solve for Tomorrow (SFT) competition by 6 March. This year, quintile 5 schools are also included, mak...

item.title: US and Israel target Iran's Assembly of Experts building amid escalating conflict
item.description: Israeli and US airstrikes have destroyed the building in Qom, Iran, where the country's next supreme leader is chosen. The attack comes after the death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran's supreme leader...

item.title: Hezbollah ready for full-scale conflict as Israel sends more troops to Lebanon
item.description: Tensions between Israel and Hezbollah have escalated as Israel sends additional troops into southern Lebanon, while Hezbollah announces its readiness for full-scale war. This follows a series of attac...

item.title: Alan Cumming Criticizes BAFTAs Broadcast Handling of Racial Slur Incident Involving Tourette's Campaigner
item.description: Actor and host Alan Cumming, who presented the 2026 BAFTA Film Awards, issued a strongly worded statement one week after the ceremony, calling the event a 'trauma triggering shitshow.' The controversy...

item.title: Iran Expands Retaliatory Strikes as Tensions with the US Continue to Escalate
item.description: Iran has broadened its retaliatory attacks on American targets across the Gulf region, with strikes on US military assets and escalating violence involving regional allies. As US-Israeli military oper...

item.title: Rapper Ghetts sentenced to 12 years for fatal hit-and-run
item.description: Rapper Ghetts, whose real name is Justin Clarke-Samuel, has been sentenced to 12 years in prison for causing the death of 20-year-old student Yubin Tamang in a hit-and-run incident last October. The c...

item.title: No Truth to Claims That Hushpuppi Has Been Released From US Prison
item.description: There has been widespread misinformation on social media claiming that Ramon Abbas, known as Hushpuppi, has been released from a United States prison and deported back to Nigeria. The false posts, whi...

item.title: Resilient Bacterium Survives Martian Impact Forces, Suggesting Life Could Transfer Between Planets
item.description: A groundbreaking experiment has shown that the famously resilient bacterium Deinococcus radiodurans can survive the crushing forces from a massive asteroid impact on Mars. This discovery opens up the ...

item.title: South Africa Hosts 2026 Africa Energy Indaba with Key Participation from Electricity and Energy Minister
item.description: The Department of Electricity and Energy, led by Minister Dr Kgosientsho Ramokgopa and Deputy Minister Graham Maré, is participating in the 18th annual Africa Energy Indaba from 3–5 March 2026 at the ...

item.title: Why Donald Trump’s Military Action in Iran Might Be More Than Just a 'Last Best Chance'
item.description: Donald Trump's administration has sparked renewed conflict with Iran, despite his previous campaign against ‘endless wars’. The rationale behind these strikes, as expressed by Trump, is that the US ha...

item.title: Navy foils P270.9-M cigarette smuggling attempt off Maguindanao
item.description: In a successful anti-smuggling operation on March 1, the Philippine Navy intercepted two boats off the coast of Maguindanao del Norte, preventing the delivery of P270.9 million worth of Indonesian-mad...

item.title: PAA Confirms Pakistan’s Airspace Remains Open and Safe for Civil Aviation
item.description: The Pakistan Airports Authority (PAA) has issued a clarification regarding the temporary unavailability of certain air routes in the Karachi and Lahore regions. The authority confirmed that Pakistan’s...

item.title: Experts Weigh In on Possible Causes of Trump's Neck Rash
item.description: On March 2, 2026, an angry rash on President Donald Trump’s neck sparked public speculation about his health. The White House confirmed that Trump is using a common skin cream as a preventative treatm...

item.title: Former Goldman Sachs Operative Deepan Pavendranathan Resurrects Elite Banking Team at Catalyst
item.description: Deepan Pavendranathan, a former Goldman Sachs operative, has brought together a team of top banking professionals at Catalyst, creating a buzz within the financial sector. Known for his expertise in i...

item.title: March 2026 Netflix Releases: New Shows, Movies, and BTS Comeback

item.title: Bill and Hillary Clinton Address Epstein Allegations, Deny Any Involvement
